key_insight: A continuous conditional diffusion model can generate realistic microstructures from process parameters using far less data by learning how to denoise conditioned noisy images.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster process-structure exploration and microstructure design for materials optimization when full physics simulations are too expensive.
method: Train a denoising diffusion model on process-microstructure pairs
Use vicinal loss to share supervision across nearby continuous process conditions
Apply classifier-free guidance and DDIM sampling for efficient conditional generation
evidence_signal: On a compact low-carbon steel dataset conditioned on manganese composition, the model reproduced key physical descriptors including phase and grain morphology, grain size distribution, phase fraction, and interfacial area distribution.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well nearby process conditions cover sparse regions, so extrapolation beyond the training range should be treated cautiously.
paper_title: Data-efficient continuous conditional denoising diffusion model for microstructure generation
